title: Mass. high court allows AG’s lawsuit against Meta to move forward
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court allows lawsuit against Meta on addictive features to proceed.
AG Andrea Campbell's suit alleges Meta's platforms harm young users via endless scrolling and algorithm-driven content.
The decision follows recent legal losses for Meta in New Mexico and California regarding user safety on platforms.

title: Why is everyone talking about polyamory?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Lindy West's memoir, 'Adult Braces,' explores polyamory and her open relationship with husband Ahamefule Oluo.
The book has sparked debate about West's happiness in polyamory, challenging her claims of contentment.
Meredith Goldstein's podcast discusses the controversy with pop culture critic Margaret Willison, sharing insights about West's influence.

title: Mass.’s new boating law is in effect. Here’s what to know.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts requires new boat safety courses for operators born after Jan. 1, 1989, effective April 1.
The Hanson-Milone Boater Safety Act mandates certification for motorboat operators, with enforcement starting in September.
Fines for non-compliance start at $50, with training available online or in-person at mass.gov.

title: Connelly Early once again gave losing Red Sox a chance to win, but failed to pitch deeper into the game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed one run and pitched 4.1 innings in Red Sox's 3-2 loss to the Cardinals.
Early's high pitch count, due to command issues, forced him out before the fifth inning finished.
Boston's reliever Zack Kelly lost the lead, resulting in a Red Sox record falling to 4-9 overall.

title: It’s been decades since a center won the NBA Most Improved Player award. Will Neemias Queta be next?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Neemias Queta's contributions help Celtics secure the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs.
Queta has doubled his playtime since joining, starting 74 of 75 games this season with 17 double-doubles.
Queta's performance positions him as a candidate for NBA Most Improved Player, a title last awarded to a center in 2002.

title: The Red Sox need to shake up the top of their lineup to avoid slow starts
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox top four batters generated just one hit against Dodgers' Dustin May on Friday night.
Manager Alex Cora considers lineup changes due to early-season struggles with current batting order.
Prospects like Mickey Gasper might be called up from Worcester to boost offensive production.

title: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference playoffs with blowout win over Pelicans
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference by defeating Pelicans 144-118 at TD Garden.
Celtics tied the NBA's single-game three-point record with 29 made threes against the Pelicans.
Jaylen Brown, managing Achilles tendinitis, scored 23 points, helping secure the playoff seeding.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Boys' Season 5 features Homelander's increasing fascism and is now streaming on Prime Video.
'Pizza Movie', starring Gaten Matarazzo and Sean Giambrone, is streaming on Hulu.
'The Testaments', set in a dystopian Boston, is based on a Margaret Atwood story and streaming on Hulu.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will not play in the Celtics' home game against the Pelicans due to right Achilles management.
Jaylen Brown is available to play despite being listed as questionable with left Achilles tendinitis.
Celtics' starters Sam Hauser, Neemias Queta, and Derrick White are probable but expected to play against New Orleans.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter's battle with cancer.
Robertson's daughter, Emma, was diagnosed with a medulloblastoma mass in her cerebellum in April 2025.
The journey inspired Robertson to run for her daughter's courage and for the support from the medical community.
